name: Test React/Vue Components in Isolation
description: Use Playwright Component Testing to mount and test individual React, Vue, or Svelte components in a real browser without running the full application.

steps.title: Install component testing dependencies
steps.description: Add the Playwright component testing package for your framework.
steps.tip: Replace 'react' with 'vue' or 'svelte' for other frameworks: @playwright/experimental-ct-vue, @playwright/experimental-ct-svelte.

steps.title: Create the component test config
steps.description: Set up a dedicated config for component tests.

steps.title: Write a component test
steps.description: Mount a component and test its behavior in a real browser.

steps.title: Run component tests
steps.description: Execute the component tests in a real browser environment.

steps.title: Test with props and slots
steps.description: Test components with different prop combinations and children.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Playwright Component Testing Setup
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Setting Up Component Testing ==="

# Detect framework
if grep -q '"react"' package.json 2>/dev/null; then
  FRAMEWORK="react"
elif grep -q '"vue"' package.json 2>/dev/null; then
  FRAMEWORK="vue"
elif grep -q '"svelte"' package.json 2>/dev/null; then
  FRAMEWORK="svelte"
else
  FRAMEWORK="react"
  echo "Framework not detected, defaulting to React"
fi

echo "Framework: $FRAMEWORK"

# Install
echo ""
echo "=== Installing Dependencies ==="
npm install -D "@playwright/experimental-ct-$FRAMEWORK"

# Create config
echo ""
echo "=== Creating Config ==="
cat > playwright-ct.config.ts << CONFIG
import { defineConfig, devices } from '@playwright/experimental-ct-$FRAMEWORK';

export default defineConfig({
  testDir: './tests/components',
  use: {
    ctPort: 3100,
  },
  projects: [
    { name: 'chromium', use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] } },
  ],
});
CONFIG

# Create test directory
mkdir -p tests/components

echo ""
echo "=== Setup Complete ==="
echo "Config: playwright-ct.config.ts"
echo "Tests:  tests/components/"
echo ""
echo "=== Commands ==="
echo "Run tests: npx playwright test -c playwright-ct.config.ts"
echo "UI mode:   npx playwright test -c playwright-ct.config.ts --ui"

faq.question: How does component testing differ from E2E testing?
faq.answer: Component testing mounts individual components in isolation without running your full application. It is faster, more focused, and tests component behavior with real browser APIs. E2E tests navigate the full application and test user workflows across multiple pages.

faq.question: Can I use component testing with Next.js?
faq.answer: Playwright component testing works best with Vite-based setups. For Next.js, you may need additional configuration. Consider using React Testing Library for unit-level component tests and Playwright for E2E tests in Next.js projects.

faq.question: How do I provide context and providers to mounted components?
faq.answer: Create a playwright/index.tsx file that wraps mounted components with your providers (ThemeProvider, QueryClientProvider, etc.). Playwright's CT setup automatically uses this wrapper for all mounted components.
